PR surface: Source +19. Total +19 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence independent reproduction was established in this checkout: the guardrail command failed before analysis because TypeScript is not installed, and current main still allowlists this file in the guard script. The PR body provides post-fix local proof for the intended guardrail failure.

target.title: fix(ci): restore cron run-log Kysely guard
target.body:
## Summary

- routes cron run-log count, page, max-seq, and prune queries through Kysely instead of raw SQLite strings
- preserves the existing filter semantics, including `not-requested` delivery status rows stored as `NULL`
- keeps this as a one-file current-main CI unblocker for the runtime schema quarantine PR

## Verification

- `node scripts/check-kysely-guardrails.mjs`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/cron/run-log/sqlite-store.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/cron/run-log/sqlite-store.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/run-log.test.ts src/cron/run-log.error-reason.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: current-main CI guardrails rejected raw SQLite usage in `src/cron/run-log/sqlite-store.ts`, which also showed up as unrelated failures on the runtime schema quarantine PR.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree with shared dependencies.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above.
Evidence after fix: Kysely guardrails reported `Kysely guardrails OK`; focused cron run-log Vitest passed 2 files / 20 tests.
Observed result after fix: the cron run-log store no longer trips the Kysely guardrail, and the focused cron behavior tests still pass.
What was not tested: full CI; this PR is intentionally scoped to the guardrail failure and focused cron tests.
target.author: vincentkoc
